name: SpectrumView Peak Marker Query
description: Enable SpectrumView on CH1, configure center frequency and span, enable the peak marker search, query marker amplitude and frequency, and compute an estimated sine-wave peak-to-peak voltage from the measured dBm value using a 50 ohm assumption.
triggers: enable spectrumview and read peak frequency
get marker amplitude and frequency from spectrumview
measure peak marker on scope spectrumview
author: Tektronix example script

handlerConfig.summary: Enable SpectrumView, configure peak marker search, query marker results, and estimate peak-to-peak voltage.

steps.label: Normalize values and estimate peak-to-peak voltage

params.code:
from math import sqrt
mkr_amp = float(str(marker_amplitude_raw).strip().strip('"'))
mkr_freq = float(str(marker_frequency_raw).strip().strip('"'))
impedance = 50.0
power = 0.001 * 10.0**(mkr_amp / 10.0)
v_rms = sqrt(power * impedance)
v_pk_pk = 2.0 * sqrt(2.0) * v_rms
print(f'Peak amplitude: {mkr_amp:.2f} dBm')
print(f'Peak frequency: {mkr_freq:.2f} Hz')
print(f'Estimated sine-wave Vpp at {impedance:.2f} ohm: {v_pk_pk:.2f} V')

name: SpectrumView Peak Marker Guidance
description: Guidance for enabling SpectrumView on CH1, configuring peak marker search, reading peak amplitude and frequency, and interpreting the result as an estimated sine-wave voltage based on a 50 ohm reference impedance.
triggers: how to use spectrumview peak markers
spectrumview marker amplitude and frequency guidance
author: Tektronix example script

handlerConfig.text: Use SpectrumView on CH1, set center frequency and span, enable peak marker search, then query the detected marker amplitude and frequency. Treat any dBm-to-voltage conversion as impedance-dependent and verify the channel termination before trusting the computed voltage.

data.summary: This provider captures a compact SpectrumView workflow: turn SpectrumView on for CH1, position the analysis window with center frequency and span, enable a peak marker search, and query the detected marker amplitude and frequency. The source also shows how to estimate sine-wave peak-to-peak voltage from dBm using a 50 ohm assumption.
sequenceHighlights: Enable SpectrumView on CH1 and wait for operation complete before applying frequency settings.
Set center frequency and span before enabling the peak marker search.
Use threshold and excursion to constrain which spectral peak is selected.
Query both amplitude and frequency after the marker search is configured.
keyPoints: The queried marker values may be returned as quoted strings and need normalization before numeric conversion.
The script interprets the amplitude as dBm and converts it to watts, RMS voltage, and then estimated peak-to-peak voltage for a sine wave.
The voltage conversion assumes a 50 ohm reference impedance.
pitfalls: Peak threshold and excursion are signal-dependent and may need adjustment for a different spectrum or noise floor.
A dBm-to-voltage conversion is only valid if the termination and reference impedance match the assumption used in the math.
The computed peak-to-peak voltage is an estimate for a sine-wave interpretation, not a universal conversion for every signal shape.
recommendedApproach: Verify the channel termination before using the converted voltage result.
Tune peak threshold and excursion based on the actual signal and noise environment.
Use the queried frequency and amplitude as the authoritative instrument outputs and treat derived voltage as post-processing.
verificationNeeded: Confirm exact SpectrumView command support and marker behavior on the target scope family.
Confirm whether the instrument returns quoted numeric strings on the target firmware.
Confirm the active input impedance before relying on the voltage conversion.
